heading: Compensatory time off for religious observances
label: 5550a Compensatory time off for religious observances
notes: References in TextThe date of enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (a), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 95–390Sept. 29, 1978
Amendments1979—Subsecs. (a), (b). substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Office” for “Commission”.Pub. L. 96–54
Effective Date of 1979 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–54section 2(b) of Pub. L. 96–54section 305 of this titleJuly 12, 1979
amendments: Amendments1979—Subsecs. (a), (b). substituted “Office of Personnel Management” for “Civil Service Commission” and “Office” for “Commission”.Pub. L. 96–54
Effective Date of 1979 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 96–54section 2(b) of Pub. L. 96–54section 305 of this titleJuly 12, 1979

children.label: a
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of the enactment of this section, the Office of Personnel Management shall prescribe regulations providing for work schedules under which an employee whose personal religious beliefs require the abstention from work during certain periods of time, may elect to engage in overtime work for time lost for meeting those religious requirements. Any employee who so elects such overtime work shall be granted equal compensatory time off from his scheduled tour of duty (in lieu of overtime pay) for such religious reasons, notwithstanding any other provision of law.

children.label: b
children.text: In the case of any agency described in subparagraphs (C) through (G) of , the head of such agency (in lieu of the Office) shall prescribe the regulations referred to in subsection (a) of this section.section 5541(1) of this title

children.label: c
children.text: Regulations under this section may provide for such exceptions as may be necessary to efficiently carry out the mission of the agency or agencies involved.
